title: OSF Naming Standard — customer-neutral identifier & token conventions (next/, DORMANT)
_status: DORMANT — descriptive data table only. Nothing live reads or validates against this file. It documents the conventions already in use (element_id / kg_merge_key / kgNodeLabel / *_ref) and pins a single reserved forward-looking alias (`equipmentPath`). No canonical or next/ schema $refs this file, so adopting it is opt-in and reversible.
_purpose: G-E naming standard. A single, plant-agnostic reference for how identifiers, labels, hierarchy-path keys and reference properties are named across SMProfiles, sources and the KG. Customer-neutral by construction: it defines the SHAPE of names, never a specific customer's plant/line/unit codes (those live only in next/examples/ demo fixtures).

principles: Customer-neutral: define the shape of a name, never a concrete plant/line/unit value. Concrete instance ids (e.g. '<site>.<area>.<cell>') belong only to demo fixtures under next/examples/.

One identity, one key: a KG node is MERGEd on its stable, owner-independent coalesce key (kg_merge_key), not on (id, owner). A telemetry source that later maps its machineId to the same key lands on the same node.

Hierarchy in the id: ISA-88/ISA-95 element ids are dot-delimited path-prefixed tokens that trace the containment chain (Enterprise -> Site -> Area -> ProcessCell -> Unit -> EquipmentModule -> ControlModule). This is the `equipmentPath` shape.
ASCII, stable, opaque-friendly: tokens are [A-Za-z0-9_-]; segments joined by '.'; no spaces, no locale-specific characters; treat ids as opaque keys, not as display text (use `name` for display).

Labels are PascalCase singular nouns (Enterprise, Site, Area, ProcessCell, EquipmentUnit, EquipmentModule, ControlModule, Machine, Equipment); relationship types are UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (PART_OF, EXECUTED_AT).

Reference properties end in `_ref` and carry the element_id of the parent/target (e.g. enterprise_ref, site_ref, process_cell_ref, equipment_module_ref); per-level identity properties end in `_id` (enterprise_id, unit_id, control_module_id).

tokens.description: Stable, opaque MERGE key for a KG node. For ISA-88/ISA-95 equipment it is path-prefixed so codes that collide across siblings stay unique. kg-builder pickId() reads it.
The cross-owner identity kg-builder coalesces on, so an OT telemetry source and the master-data anchor land on ONE node. The anchor-loader sets kg_merge_key = element_id.

tokens.role: RESERVED forward-looking ALIAS of element_id / kg_merge_key
tokens.description: A named, self-documenting alias for the dot-delimited containment path. DORMANT: not emitted, not consumed, not validated anywhere today. When adopted it will be the canonical, human-meaningful name for the same value element_id/kg_merge_key already carry — purely additive, no migration of stored ids.
Primary Neo4j label the profile maps its instances to. Customer-neutral TYPE name, never an instance name.
The profile-level property that carries this node's identity within its level.

tokens.role: reference property to a parent/target node
tokens.description: Carries the element_id of the related node; used by relationships[].targetIdProp to draw PART_OF / EXECUTED_AT edges.
Edge type stamped by kg-builder safeRelType(). PART_OF for containment, EXECUTED_AT for order->equipment.
